lesson: A machine that's completely dead, with nothing in the log and everything at room temperature, isn't broken — something upstream cut the power. Check the wall before you check the machine.
symptoms: It's completely dead. Won't even turn on.
No display, no lights, no hum.

tests.temp-probe: Bowl at room temperature throughout. Nothing in the machine has been cold for hours.

symptoms: Dead as a doornail since we opened.
The drinks fridge on the same bench is also off.

flavour: The cleaner's plug-in timer strikes again. Invoice includes a small fee for keeping a straight face.
authenticityNote: The GDD §2.1 red-herring class made Tier 1: shops put cleaning equipment on plug-in wall timers and the machine ends up on the switched socket. Techs genuinely get called to machines that are just switched off at the wall — a log ending abruptly with no shutdown event is the classic tell.
